---
title: "Cybersecurity Hackathon"
page-layout: custom
title-block-style: none
toc: false
subtitle: "48-Hour Challenge for Security Enthusiasts"
description: "Compete in a 48-hour cybersecurity hackathon on June 15-16, 2025 in Cluj-Napoca, designed for security enthusiasts of all skill levels."
date: "2025-06-15"
enddate: "2025-06-16"
location: "Innovation Hub, Cluj-Napoca"
category: "hackathon"
image: "/assets/images/cyber-security.jpg"
registration_date: "2025-04-01"
include-in-header:
  text: |
    <style>.m { --c: #F5A623; }</style>
---

```{=html}
<div class="m">

<div class="m-hero">
  <div class="container">
    <h1>Cybersecurity <span class="gr">Hackathon</span></h1>
    <p>48-Hour Challenge for Security Enthusiasts</p>
  </div>
</div>

<div class="m-s">
  <div class="container">
    <div class="event-dates">
      <span class="badge bg-primary">June 15-16, 2025</span>
      <div class="event-actions">
        <a href="/events/ical/cybersecurity-hackathon-2025.ics" class="btn btn-outline-primary btn-sm" download>
          <i class="bi bi-calendar3"></i> Add to Calendar
        </a>
        <a href="/events/pdfs/cybersecurity-hackathon.pdf" class="btn btn-outline-secondary btn-sm" download>
          <i class="bi bi-file-pdf"></i> Download PDF
        </a>
      </div>
    </div>
    <div class="event-location">
      <i class="bi bi-geo-alt"></i> Innovation Hub, Cluj-Napoca
    </div>
  </div>
</div>

<div class="m-bg">
  <div class="container">

<h2>Event Overview</h2>

<p>The Cybersecurity Hackathon is a 48-hour challenge where participants work in teams to solve real-world cybersecurity problems. Open to security enthusiasts of all levels, from beginners to experts, this event provides a platform to develop and showcase your skills while networking with industry professionals.</p>

<h2>Challenge Format</h2>

<p>Teams of 2-4 participants will tackle a series of increasingly difficult cybersecurity challenges across multiple domains. The event combines elements of Capture The Flag (CTF) competitions with practical security implementation scenarios that mirror real-world situations.</p>

<h3>Challenge Categories</h3>

<ul>
<li><strong>Web Application Security</strong>: Identify and exploit vulnerabilities in web applications</li>
<li><strong>Network Security</strong>: Analyze and secure network infrastructure</li>
<li><strong>Cryptography</strong>: Break and implement cryptographic systems</li>
<li><strong>Reverse Engineering</strong>: Analyze compiled applications to understand their functionality</li>
<li><strong>Forensics</strong>: Investigate digital evidence to reconstruct security incidents</li>
<li><strong>Social Engineering</strong>: Develop strategies to counter social engineering attacks</li>
</ul>

<h2>Schedule</h2>

<h3>Day 1 (June 15)</h3>

<table class="table">
<thead><tr><th>Time</th><th>Activity</th></tr></thead>
<tbody>
<tr><td>08:00 - 09:00</td><td>Registration and Check-in</td></tr>
<tr><td>09:00 - 10:00</td><td>Opening Ceremony and Challenge Briefing</td></tr>
<tr><td>10:00 - 12:00</td><td>Team Formation and Strategy Session</td></tr>
<tr><td>12:00 - 13:00</td><td>Lunch</td></tr>
<tr><td>13:00 - 18:00</td><td>Hacking Session</td></tr>
<tr><td>18:00 - 19:00</td><td>Dinner</td></tr>
<tr><td>19:00 - 22:00</td><td>Evening Workshop: "Advanced Exploitation Techniques"</td></tr>
<tr><td>22:00 - 08:00</td><td>Overnight Hacking (Optional)</td></tr>
</tbody>
</table>

<h3>Day 2 (June 16)</h3>

<table class="table">
<thead><tr><th>Time</th><th>Activity</th></tr></thead>
<tbody>
<tr><td>08:00 - 09:00</td><td>Breakfast</td></tr>
<tr><td>09:00 - 12:00</td><td>Continued Hacking Session</td></tr>
<tr><td>12:00 - 13:00</td><td>Lunch</td></tr>
<tr><td>13:00 - 15:00</td><td>Final Hacking Sprint</td></tr>
<tr><td>15:00 - 16:00</td><td>Solution Submission Deadline</td></tr>
<tr><td>16:00 - 17:30</td><td>Team Presentations</td></tr>
<tr><td>17:30 - 18:30</td><td>Judging and Deliberation</td></tr>
<tr><td>18:30 - 20:00</td><td>Awards Ceremony and Closing</td></tr>
</tbody>
</table>

<h2>Prizes and Recognition</h2>

<ul>
<li><strong>First Place</strong>: &euro;5,000 and mentorship opportunities with leading cybersecurity firms</li>
<li><strong>Second Place</strong>: &euro;2,500 and premium security tools licenses</li>
<li><strong>Third Place</strong>: &euro;1,000 and security conference tickets</li>
<li><strong>Category Winners</strong>: Special recognition in each challenge category</li>
<li><strong>Most Innovative Solution</strong>: Special award for the most creative approach</li>
</ul>

<h2>Who Should Participate</h2>

<ul>
<li>Security professionals looking to sharpen their skills</li>
<li>Computer science students interested in cybersecurity careers</li>
<li>Software developers wanting to improve their security knowledge</li>
<li>IT professionals seeking to enhance their security capabilities</li>
<li>Security researchers and enthusiasts of all levels</li>
</ul>

<h2>Registration Information</h2>

<p>Registration opens on April 1, 2025. Early registration is encouraged as participation is limited to 100 participants (25-30 teams) to ensure quality mentoring and support.</p>

  </div>
</div>

<div class="m-cta">
  <div class="container">
    <div class="m-d"></div>
    <h2>Ready to Test Your Cybersecurity Skills?</h2>
    <p>Join us for an intense 48-hour hackathon that will challenge your abilities and connect you with the cybersecurity community.</p>
    <a href="https://cal.com/chenist" target="_blank" class="m-b">Register Interest</a>
  </div>
</div>

<div class="m-bg">
  <div class="container">

<h2>Frequently Asked Questions</h2>

<h3>Do I need to be a cybersecurity expert to participate?</h3>
<p>No, we welcome participants of all skill levels. Teams are encouraged to include members with diverse experience levels, and mentors will be available to assist throughout the event.</p>

<h3>What should I bring?</h3>
<p>Participants should bring their own laptops, chargers, and any preferred peripherals. We'll provide power, high-speed internet, and a comfortable workspace.</p>

<h3>Is there a participation fee?</h3>
<p>Yes, there is a nominal fee of &euro;50 per participant to cover event costs, meals, and materials. Student discounts are available with valid ID.</p>

<h3>Will accommodation be provided?</h3>
<p>Accommodation is not included, but we have arranged special rates with nearby hotels. Details will be provided to registered participants.</p>

<h3>Can I participate remotely?</h3>
<p>This hackathon is designed as an in-person event to maximize collaboration and mentorship opportunities. Remote participation is not available.</p>

  </div>
</div>

</div>
```
